The decorator you type ten times a day is no longer how Angular wants you to write components.
@Input. @Output. The first two things you ever learned about Angular.
Both have replacements now. Function-based, signal-powered, and officially the recommended path.
I migrated a 120-component app to the new style over two sprints. Some migrations took thirty seconds. One of them silently changed behavior and cost me an afternoon.
Here are all six migrations, the exact before and after for each, the one that bites, and the numbers I measured when the migration met OnPush and zoneless.
If you write Angular components, this is not optional reading anymore. New Angular defaults to OnPush, and signal inputs are how components stay fast in that world.
Why Angular Replaced Its Two Most Famous Decorators
The old decorators have a problem you stopped noticing years ago: a plain property tells nobody when it changes.
OLD WORLD NEW WORLD @Input() user: User; user = input<User>();
| |
